metadata.title: Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan

abstract: Child maltreatment is a global issue deeply rooted in cultural, economic, and social practices. The present study investigates the representation of child abuse in the mainstream newspapers of Pakistan (i.e., Dawn and The News) from 2013 to 2020. This specific time frame is that these cases increased during these years. The data regarding the child abuse cases were accessed from the LexisNexis database. A computational technique of topic modeling (Latent Dirichlet Allocations) was applied to discover the hidden topics from the filtered data. The study underpinned Computational Grounded Theory as a theoretical frame for this study. The results reveal topics related to awareness of child abuse, the types of child abuse, and the law and procedural matters specific to child abuse cases in Pakistan. The study brings possible implications that child protection laws are to be improved to overcome the menace of child abuse in Pakistan.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: Child maltreatment is defined as an act or series of acts of commission or omission by parents or caretakers which results in potential harm or threat of injury to a child; it is a global issue deeply rooted in cultural, economic, and social practices (Gilbert et al., 2009;Higgins, 2004). Child maltreatment is a heterogeneous phenomenon that refers to four different caretaking causalities; physical abuse, sexual abuse, physical neglect, and emotional mistreatment (Starr & Wolfe, 1991). An aggressive home environment and the consequences of child abuse and neglect result in shortand long-term hostile effects on the children's social, physical, and mental well-being.

Referring to the context of Pakistan, the issue of child abuse increased drastically. The NGO Ahmed & Waqas (2022), which works on child abuse, releases annual reports highlighting the cases reported in Pakistan. The yearly reports comprising the data from 2013 to 2020 show a projection in child abuse. It also provides news representation in local and mainstream newspapers in Urdu and English languages.

The statistics extracted from the annual reports reveal that situation of child abuse in Pakistan is quite alarming as shown in Figure 1. Keeping in view, the increase shown in Figure 1, the present study selected the time frame from 2013 to 2020 to investigate how the issue of child abuse has been represented in the mainstream English newspapers of Pakistan. Moreover, the study aims to highlight the causes and factors related to the problem of child abuse.

On the other hand, topic modeling has been applied to the data extracted from newspapers regarding child abuse in Pakistan. Topic modeling is a robust algorithm technique that filters topics from a large corpus of textual data (Qiang et al., 2022;Ramage et al., 2009). It helps get the topics and to understand the nature of the issues. The use of topic modeling in social sciences has increased as it offers a better understanding of the large volume of the data spanning over a more extended period. The present research is aimed to discover the hidden topics regarding child abuse by applying the topic modeling technique. It also aims to investigate how the linguistics constructions reveal the portrayal of child maltreatment in the newspapers of Pakistan using topic modeling.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: Child abuse is a heterogeneous phenomenon that has rapidly increased around the world. Children are maltreated at home, in schools, markets, and in industries regardless of class, group, religion, and gender (van IJzendoorn et al., 2020). The prominent types of abuse are physical, emotional, and sexual abuse. According to Crawford (2010), physical abuse is defined as any behavior that Figure 1. The Data Obtained from Sahil Annual Reports (Ahmed & Waqas, 2022).

causes a child to sustain an injury (hair pulling, hitting) and neglect includes failing to meet a child's physical and emotional requirements. Apart from physical, emotional abuse involves behaviors that make them feel unloved, unvalued, and undesired (insulting, shouting) (Rees, 2010). Another type of abuse is sexual, such as genital or oral contact, child exhibitionism, and child pornography are all examples of sexual abuse (Barth et al., 2013).

The consequences of child abuse in children are enormous. Kendall-Tackett (2014) reveals that child maltreatment can lead to post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), anxiety, low self-esteem, social dysfunction, interpersonal issues, high-risk behavior, suicide, and violence as an adult. Stirling and Amaya-Jackson ( 2008) 's research on child abuse consequences reveals that children's early abuse or neglect have significant behavior problems of instability, depression, and violence. On the other hand, child abuse also affects economic well-being at later stages (Currie & Spatz Widom, 2010). More child maltreatment has more adverse effects on the probability of employment in middle age, especially for females. On the other hand, the clinical consequences of child abuse have a significant impact on disease vulnerability and poor treatment response to mood disorders (Lippard & Nemeroff, 2020). Parker and Nemeroff (2021)' s research on child abuse and neglect reveal that stress and trauma contribute to medical and psychiatric disorders. In short, the consequences of child abuse can be summed up as having both short-term and long-term effects on victims. The long-term effects are alarming as they affect the life progress of victims for a long.

Focusing on child abuse in Pakistan, Child abuse cases are under-reported because of society's sensitivity, which prevents children from talking about what they have endured (Avais et al., 2020). Although taking deliberate steps toward handling child sexual abuse is considered a taboo in society (Abbas & Jabeen, 2020). In addition, the victims' families are not willing to report anywhere because of the fear of shame for the act and the discrimination, and social segregation they face in the community and are stigmatized for the rest of their lives (Evans et al., 2021;Fukushima et al., 2020). Therefore, pressure builds not to disclose their child's sexual abuse to protect the family from the stigma associated with reporting the crime. Due to these factors, Pakistan's number of reported crimes is relatively less (Ahmed & Waqas, 2022;Mehnaz, 2018). Hence, many child abuse cases go unheard due to social stigma and cultural shame.

Moreover, state infrastructure for child protection rights needs much improvement. According to the child courts book, every district in Pakistan should have a Juvenile court. But the ground reality is quite different (Mughal, 2021). According to Jabeen (2016), there are three significant challenges: a) the challenge of defining the concept of child protection for practice, b) the establishment of formal administrative and institutional structures, and c) the need for systematic effort to cope with the environment of a societal reluctance. The child protection system in Pakistan centers on children and justice, preventing commercial and sexual exploitation, protecting children without adequate family care, and neglecting psychological care (Khan et al., 2013). Hence, psychological trauma and neglect that a child can face are not the centers of focus in the child rights system in Pakistan. On the other hand, legislators and policymakers also find hurdles when considering Pakistan's child protection system. "One of the broader issues blocking effective child protection in Pakistan is its cultural norms and beliefs" (Pulla et al., 2018, p. 65).

On the other hand, topic modeling is a viable and robust method for dealing with textual data (Blei, 2012). It reduces the complexity of the language to a simplistic assumption, namely that certain words often occur together, and these "co-occurances" or word clusters carry traces of meaning which help in the meaning-making process (Ylä-Anttila et al., 2021). Topic modeling has been applied in social sciences to extract latent topics from a large corpus. It has helped investigate the issues relating to economics (F. Ahmed, Nawaz, et al., 2022), climate change (Ylä-Anttila et al., 2021), policymaking (Isoaho et al., 2021;Ylä-Anttila et al., 2021), framing discourses (F. Ahmed, Mubeen, et al., 2022), countries' priority settings (Baturo et al., 2017) . The primary topic modeling method is LDA, a probabilistic model that models the probability of each topic or word cluster in each document and the likelihood of each word in each topic (Blei, 2012). In social sciences, the validity of LDA is essential to be considered. Nowlin (2016) recommends semantic validity and construct validity for LDA. Semantic validity refers to how the terms are associated with each topic, and construct validity refers to how the topics correspond to the exogenous events.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: Topic modeling refers to a combination of computational algorithms that aims to discover the main themes that pervade a large and other unstructured collection of documents (Blei, 2012). "With a collection of documents as input, a topic model can produce a set of interpretable topics (i.e., the group of words that are associated under a single theme) and assesses the strength with which each document exhibits those topics" (DiMaggio et al., 2013, p. 577). Topic modeling helps find patterns in the datasets. In social research, meaning-making is the prime essence as it informs what different aspects of meaning are prevalent in a text (F. Ahmed, Nawaz, et al., 2022). In contrast to traditional close reading techniques, topic modeling reduces the complexity of the language to the simplistic assumption that certain words occur together, and these co-occurrencesword clusters carry traces of meaning (Ylä-Anttila et al., 2021).

We use LDA (Latent Dirichlet Allocations) to apply topic modeling. LDA applies a relational approach to meaning because co-occurrences are essential in assignment words or topics (DiMaggio et al., 2013). As topic modeling is used following unsupervised machine learning, researchers give no input at the initial stage. The topic modeling classifies the text based on cooccurrences or "habitual words" (Ylä-Anttila et al., 2021). Research suggests that different mixedmethods research designs are appropriate for textual analysis through topic modeling (Isoaho et al., 2021). We adopted the abductive approach, which combines both inductive and deductive methods. Abduction refers to a creative inferential process to produce new hypotheses and theories based on surprising research evidence (Timmermans & Tavory, 2012). We also applied subject-specific researchers' knowledge after conducting the primary inductive classification through topic modeling. This helps us understand the social and cultural aspects of the matter under investigation regarding topic modeling (DiMaggio et al., 2013).

It is essential to mention that we used Python programming to apply LDA for this study. For LDA, we used different libraries that supported filtering and analyzing the data. Those libraries are NLTK, SpaCy, Gensim, Matplotlib Pyplot, and Scikit-learn. The NLTK library was used for data filtration such as stemming, lemmatization, and stopwords removal. SpaCy and Gensim libraries have been used explicitly for employing LDA for this study. Lastly, Matplotlib has been applied to visualize the results such as LDA visualization, word coulds, and Scikit-learn library for the t-SNE distribution of topics.

paragraphs: The data based on child abuse was extracted from the two mainstream Pakistani English newspapers from 2013 to 2020. The rationale for selecting the specific period has been given in the paper's introductory section. We selected the headlines of the news articles. The rationale for selecting the news headlines is that they contain the gist of all the information and possess equal effect compared to the newspapers' explicit content (Condit et al., 2001).

Table 1 explains that 1193 news headlines appeared in the mainstream newspapers from 2013 to 2020. Tang et al. (2014), using statistical measures to the limit of topic modeling, recommend documents of at least 100-200 words in length, while the number of documents needs to be at least 1000-2000 provided that the sets of vocabulary associated with different topics are separated. In the case of the present study, the length of the documents in the form of headings may vary, but the number of headlines is well above the recommended value. Though the recommendation is 100-200 words in length which seems not fulfilling in the case of headlines. However, headlines being the gist of the information, have been used as the subject of study in topic modeling in various other studies (F. Ahmed, Nawaz, et al., 2022;Ghasiya & Okamura, 2021). In addition, short text topic modeling techniques are also in fashion to investigate the topics from short texts (Albalawi et al., 2020;Li et al., 2018;Qiang et al., 2022;Zuo et al., 2016).

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: The data filtration helps in filtering the relevant text. It is the essential part of the validity of the text processing in topic modeling. Initially, the data were accessed from LexisNexis database. It is a database that provides data from different sources, including the world-leading newspapers (Weaver & Bimber, 2008). The following initial filters were set to extract the data regarding child abuse in Pakistan. Table 2 explains the time, keywords, and sources used to extract the data on the relevant topic.
In addition, the downloaded data were combined into a single file. The variation in the data from both sources was ensured.

The second stage was the pre-processing of the data. If the pre-processing parameters have been incorrectly set, a generated topic may be an amalgam of multiple distinct topics (Isoaho et al., 2021). Therefore, the pre-processing phase was conducted. Text pre-processing is an essential part of any NLP system since the characters, words, and sentences identified at this stage are fundamental (Kannan et al., 2014). In this step, the paralinguistic features were removed. Hyperlinks, file headers, markups, and metadata are paralinguistic features (Famili et al., 1997). Removing paralinguistic features is that "they make the data noisy which later becomes a hurdle to extract the hidden meanings from the textual data" (F. Ahmed, Mubeen, et al., 2022). The second step of preprocessing involved tokenization. It is the phase of defining the word and sentence boundaries (Kannan et al., 2014). After that, the process of stemming was conducted. Stemming helps eliminate inflected word forms into a single word. For example, "abusing," "abused," and "abusive" were all converted to the stem word "abuse." Later, normalization was followed. All the text was converted to lower case, and all the punctuations were removed. Subsequently, the stop words were exempted from the data. These are words that have been defined by linguists regarding different languages and contribute less to the meaning-making process. Moreover, the stop words do not contribute to the context or content of the textual documents (Kannan et al., 2014). The stop words in English are 179. The list of stop words was extended, keeping a view of the nature of the data. The names of cities, people, and places were also added to the list of the stop words. It helped in reducing the noise in the data. Before running the LDA, bigrams, and trigrams were also treated as single terms. The terms that occurred more than 100 times in the corpus as a combination two-word or three-word cluster were analyzed as single bigram or trigram. It helped in further filtering the relevant data in the corpus. Topic modeling also provides to select the specific syntax categories to understand better a topic extracted from a corpus. We only preserved nouns, adjectives, and verb categories to extract the meaning.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: The topics generated through LDA, especially in social sciences, are essential to be seen from the lenses of interpretability. The generated topics have been interpreted following the rationality of meaning, contextual polysemy, and semantic validation of the model (DiMaggio et al., 2013). Meaning from the generated topics was regarded as relational and not dependent on words, icons, or symbols. It helped in finding out correct labels for the generated topics through LDA. On the other hand, contextual polysemy justified using a word in multiple contexts. Applying contextual polysemy, the presence of one word in numerous generated topics helped clarify the position of that word regarding those topics. Lastly, comparing the labels with their respective generated topics proved the semantic validation of the model.

Another part of internal validation that has been specific to this study has been employed in topic labeling through the topic visualization process. The topic visualization provides 30 keywords of the same topic displayed in Table 3 with ten keywords. In the case of some topics (such as topic 2 in Table 3) extracted through LDA, it was difficult to label the topic due to the limited number of keywords. However, with 30 keywords in context through topic visualization, it became easier for us to label the topic.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: The study underpins Computational Grounded Theory (CGT), the extended form of the grounded theory that meets the evolving needs concerning grounded theory. The main feature of CGT is that it combines expert human knowledge with the processing power and pattern recognition of computers. So in this process, the research is blind and applied topic modeling with no prior assumption. This process of topic modeling brings it near to Grounded theory. The revised form of grounded theory has been proposed concerning computational techniques referred to as computational grounded theory (Ophir et al., 2020). The computational grounded theory combines expert human knowledge and hermeneutic skills with computers' processing power and pattern recognition. It involves three main steps a) pattern detection, that is, computational inductive exploration of the text, b) pattern refinement, that is, interpretive engagement of the data, and c) pattern confirmation step, that is, assess inductively identified patterns (Nelson, 2020).

Table 3 explains the groups of most co-occurring words ranging from 0 to 9. Each group consists of 10 keywords with various frequencies from higher to lower, referred to as "words' weightage" (Blei, 2012). The themes on the right-hand side of Table 3 have been proposed to provide a clear picture of the extracted keywords.

The first set of keywords in Table 3 relates to awareness (0.025) regarding child abuse at the school level (0.028). To protect (0.075) children from child abuse and violence (0.048), there is a need to create awareness (0.025). The lack (0.019) of the proper system (0.027) at the school level is one of the reasons for child abuse. In this set of keywords, the word "protect" (0.075) has the highest weightage, implying children's protection against child abuse in Pakistan.

The second set of keywords relates to child abuse and drugs (0.044). The use of drugs in children drives children towards crimes (0.035) and scandals (0.045). Drugs at this stage affect their bodies. The rise in the use of drugs has been reported to increase on a monthly (0.025) basis.

Procedural Delays in Child Abuse Cases

The third set of keywords refers to child labor (0.033). It is the fundamental right (0.095) of children not to force to work. The state should introduce a child labor policy to prevent the forceful labor of children. The changes (0.029) in the existing child protection laws are necessary. It can avert child labor on a massive scale. The term "right (0.095)" has the highest weightage in this group which implies that it is the fundamental right of children not to be forced into labor at this stage.

The fourth set of keywords emphasizes child protection (0.066) laws. With the increasing cases of child abuse, the protests on society levels urged (0.028) to set (0.026) victims (0.017) on trials under these new laws. A campaign (0.014) on a societal level is necessary for this cause. The term "protection (0.066)" has the highest weightage, implying that child security is a prime factor in this group of keywords.

The fifth set of keywords revolves around child rape (0.059) in Pakistan, especially the reported cases highlight the increasing number of minor (0.042) rapes on a yearly (0.037) basis. The students (0.044) at the school level are also victims of child abuse. The rape of two minor girls in two different cities in Pakistan launch a strong protest in society (N. Ahmed & Kamran, 2021;Chughtai, 2018;Rahim et al., 2021). The keyword rape (0.059) carries the highest weightage, followed by the keyword students (0.044) implies that rape cases with minor (0.042) students in Pakistan are a severe issue and need to be dealt with on a prior basis.

The sixth set of keywords relates to domestic violence. This domestic violence, based on data, can be divided into two types. The first is violence on the part of family members, and the second is violence with children working as servants in homes. The keywords suggest making both girls (0.047) and boys (0.034) safe from domestic violence. Regardless of gender bases, children are assaulted (0.027) (Malik & Rizvi, 2009). This highlights (0.018) that due to the lack of child protection laws, children face domestic violence at home.

The seventh set of keywords revolves around murderer (0.045) proceedings. The culprits (0.021) of the murder take (0.018) advantage of the volatile law proceedings and seldom get sentences in these cases. The social (0.025) protest through a different medium (0.032) vanishes quickly by revisiting the data of the newspapers' articles, the following content collocates with the word murder. The need of the hour for Pakistan is to rebuild the way we contend with criminals and crimes.

The eighth set of keywords relates to the awareness of child abuse through media. The keywords relate to a drama (0.019) serial (0.021) titled "Udari," which became famous due to the case of child abuse. It reveals the outcome of child abuse in the form of suicide (0.016). It opened debates about many cultural taboos which are not discussed openly due to social pressure.

The next set of keywords relates to child abuse legal proceedings. The reported sexual (0.018) abuse cases took longer than expected for their conclusion. sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: The study investigated the issue of child abuse through its representation in the print media in Pakistan. The newspaper data based on child abuse was extracted from 2013 to 2020. The study applied LDA to extract topics from the newspaper corpus. A total of ten topics emerged from the newspaper corpus. Overall, the topics extracted from the textual data include awareness about child abuse, child abuse and drugs, child labor, child rape, domestic violence, children murderers, awareness through media, law proceedings, and procedural delays in child abuse cases.

The study underpins computational grounded theory, which states three main steps, that is, pattern detection, pattern refinement, and pattern confirmation (Nelson, 2020). In this process, the inductive computational exploration of the text has been applied. It is similar to grounded theory in that the meaning is grounded in the text, which is explored through inductive computation using computational techniques such as LDA without any prior assumptions or hypotheses about a research topic.

Based on the extracted topics explained in Table 3, all the extracted topics can be divided into three main layers regarding child abuse in Pakistan. The first layer is related to awareness about child abuse. This awareness further can be categorized as awareness at school (i.e., Topic 0) level and awareness through media (i.e., Topic 7), as explained in Table 3. Awareness at the school level has been emphasized because multiple cases of child abuse have been reported (Jabeen, 2021;Rahim et al., 2021). Studies suggest that the role of educators in averting child abuse is more pivotal and radical, especially during the early education system (Rafique et al., 2020). On the other hand, awareness through media plays a crucial role in facilitating community education and child abuse prevention. Media influences the community to raise awareness for children's rights (Nair, 2019;Saunders & Goddard, 2002). Media is a potential tool to mobilize society for abuse prevention and child rights (Goddard & Saunders, 2001).

The second layer deals with the core areas of child abuse. Child abuse through drugs (i.e., Topic 1), child labor (i.e., Topic 2), child rape (i.e., Topic 4), and domestic violence (i.e., Topic 5) are topics that relate to child abuse as explained in Table 3. First, the children are abused through the use of drugs. Studies suggest that child abuse and neglect significantly increase the use of drugs in children, resulting in subsequent depression and anxiety (Iqbal, 2008;Sherman et al., 2005;Sperry & Widom, 2013;Widom & Hiller-Sturmhöfel, 2001). Child labor is the second prominent topic that emerged from the data. The main reason for children in Pakistan is poverty. Studies reveal that children from 5 to 14 are forced to do work to earn their lives (Fatima, 2017;Jafri & Raishad, 1997). The other aspect relating to child abuse is child rape cases in Pakistan. Comparing the representation of rape cases with the annual reports issued by NGO Ahmed & Waqas, 2022 reveals a big difference which leads to under-representation of the child rape cases in Pakistan (Jabeen, 2020;Riaz, 2021). The need is to make an effective policy to prevent child sexual abuse (Gillani, 2009). Studies also reveal that fewer of these cases are reported due to cultural shame in Pakistan. However, the cases which have been reported and highlighted by the media demand the role of effective policy for child protection. The third category of child abuse from the extracted topics relates to domestic violence. Domestic violence leads to psychological maltreatment, and the children in these cases are at high risk of physical or sexual abuse (Herrenkohl et al., 2008;Holden, 2003). Studies based on domestic violence in Pakistan suggest introducing domestic and physical violence in the public health policy in Pakistan (Fikree & Bhatti, 1999;Saleem et al., 2021;Zakar et al., 2016). The topic emerged from the child abuse corpus from 2013 to 2020, implying that the reported cases of child abuse in newspapers relate mainly to these four topics explained above. The need of the time is to review the situation and introduce policy measures for child protection in Pakistan.

The third layer deals with the legal proceedings of the child abuse cases in Pakistan. Topic 3, 6, 8, and 9 relate to the aspect of legal proceedings-child protection laws which are necessary to overcome the issue of child abuse. Studies on the Pakistani context reveal that it lacks internationally accepted rights-based on child protection mechanisms at the national and provincial levels (Ul Mustafa & Mehmood, 2015). Moreover, a security-focused narrative and socioeconomic realities such as poverty are the main priorities of policymakers, which hinders child protection laws (Abbas, 2021). On the other hand, studies based on the context in Pakistan suggest that the lack of adequate state structures paves the way for child abusers to avoid penalties (Granich et al., 2021). Topics 8 and 9 refer to child abuse law proceedings and procedural delays in child abuse cases which implies that the delay in these cases becomes why the child abuse cases are not reported (Hussain, 2011;Jabeen, 2016).

Some recent developments have positively impacted bringing child abuse cases to justice in Pakistan. A significant development is DNA profiling, which helps solve multiple child abuse cases in Pakistan (Arif et al., 2019;Mont & White, 2013;Shahid et al., 2021). Studies suggest that DNA profiling approaches are more effective than traditional approaches to the investigation (Doleac, 2017;Heinemann et al., 2016). The need of the time is to focus on advanced methods of inquiry to overcome the problem of child abuse, especially in the context of Pakistan.

Studies on the Pakistani context reveal that it lacks internationally accepted rights-based on child protection mechanisms at the national and provincial levels (Ul Mustafa & Mehmood, 2015). Moreover, a security-focused narrative and socioeconomic realities such as poverty are the main priorities of policymakers, which hinders child protection laws (Abbas, 2021). On the other hand, studies based on the context in Pakistan suggest that the lack of adequate state structures paves the way for child abusers to avoid penalties (Granich et al., 2021). Topics 8 and 9 refer to child abuse law proceedings and procedural delays in child abuse cases which implies that the delay in these cases becomes why the child abuse cases are not reported (Hussain, 2011;Jabeen, 2016).

Some recent developments have positively impacted bringing child abuse cases to justice in Pakistan. A significant development is DNA profiling, which helps solve multiple child abuse cases in Pakistan (Arif et al., 2019;Mont & White, 2013;Shahid et al., 2021). Studies suggest that DNA profiling approaches are more effective than traditional approaches to the investigation (Doleac, 2017;Heinemann et al., 2016). The need of the time is to focus on advanced methods of inquiry to overcome the problem of child abuse, especially in the context of Pakistan.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: Based on the topics extracted and the discussion on these topics, the study concludes that the first step is to create awareness. School education and media are the main potential tools to create awareness. Through schools, children can be made aware of the situation where they can protect themselves and raise their voices. Through media, parents, and society can be educated on protecting children. The maltreatment of children can be thwarted through protection at the family and societal level in Pakistan. Awareness in the community may cause movements for child rights.

The second step is to identify the forms of abuses being committed. Based on the extracted topics, it can be concluded that the use of drugs, labor in childhood, child rape, and domestic violence are the primary forms of child abuse in Pakistan. The maltreatment of children drives them toward seclusion, which results in addiction in children. Similarly, children are forced to work due to extreme poverty, depriving them of their fundamental right to education. In this case, policy measures are indispensable. Child rape is another form of violence that results in the murder of children in Pakistan. From 2013 to 2020, multiple cases of rape have been reported. The case of Zainab's rape and murder raised many concerns about protecting children in Pakistan. Lastly, domestic violence in Pakistan causes child abuse cases. Children protection laws need to be seen, keeping in view the above forms of child abuse in Pakistan.

The third step is to improve the law proceedings that hinder the victims' justice. Mechanism to improve child protection laws at the national and provincial levels is necessary to bring changes in existing laws. Child abuse cases need to be prioritized by the state.

The possible implications for the study may be related to the three areas pointed out in the discussion section. Firstly, the child rights policy on the part of the state is to be introduced. Awareness regarding child abuse may be raised at the school level. A possible way may be aware the children through their textbooks. Second, the laws for child protection need to be reviewed to make them more docile to protect the victims which may help the people come forward to report the cases of child abuse in Pakistan.

sections.paper_id: Ahmed and Asim - 2023 - Topic Modeling as a Tool to Analyze Child Abuse from the Corpus of English Newspapers in Pakistan
paragraphs: This study encompasses possible limitations and future research directions. The first limitation is that the data on child abuse cases have been taken from 2013 to 2020 from the English newspapers in Pakistan. In order to have a clearer picture of the child abuse cases, the recent data for the years 2021-2022 can provide may be beneficial. Hence, future research direction is to consider the data on a longer timeline to have an in-depth understanding of child abuse in Pakistan.

The second limitation of the study relates to the data extracted from mainstream newspapers in Pakistan. The present data regarding child abuse cases only includes newspaper headlines. Based on this limitation, a possible future research direction is to investigate child abuse cases, considering the detailed content of the newspapers.

The third limitation is that the number of cases reported through newspapers is less than the actual instances in Pakistan (Ahmed & Waqas, 2022). The reason is the cultural shame and stigma that the victims face on the part of society (Mehnaz, 2018). However, these data representation in newspapers may be taken as a sample for all the cases in Pakistan. The other reason is that some of these cases have been reported in local newspapers in their local languages that do not reach mainstream newspapers (Ahmed & Waqas, 2022;Avais et al., 2020).
